_doc: Compiled from Dietary_Context_Inference.md — CLR ratio dietary pattern inference
core_principle: Metabolic signatures suggest dietary patterns but cannot confirm actual intake. Pattern → hypothesis, not pattern → confirmed diet.

CUR_low.likely_diet: Low fiber, high protein OR inadequate fermentable fiber
CUR_low.intervention: Increase fermentable fiber substrates, consider phased protein modulation

CUR_balanced.intervention: Check other ratios for specific bottlenecks

FCR_low.likely_diet: Single-fiber source dominance OR rapidly fermentable fiber excess
consistent_with: Probiotic use with high lactate strains
FCR_low.intervention: Diversify fiber sources, avoid adding more of same fiber type

MDR_high.intervention: GRADUAL fiber reintroduction — start with low-fermentability fibers

PPR_high.intervention: Increase fiber (dilutes protein substrate), consider protein modulation

western_diet.description: High protein, low fiber, refined carbohydrates
western_diet.client_summary: Your metabolic pattern suggests a diet higher in protein and lower in fiber-rich plant foods than ideal for your gut bacteria.

plant_forward_optimal.signature: CUR > +0.5 AND FCR > +0.3 AND MDR < -0.5 AND PPR < -0.5
plant_forward_optimal.description: High fiber diversity, moderate protein, plant-forward
plant_forward_optimal.client_summary: Your metabolic pattern is consistent with a varied, plant-rich diet that provides diverse fibers — this is ideal for gut health.

supplement_driven_monoculture.signature: CUR > +0.3 AND FCR < -0.3 AND Cross-Feeders low
supplement_driven_monoculture.description: High single-fiber supplement use creating narrow fermentation
supplement_driven_monoculture.client_summary: Your gut bacteria are getting plenty of fiber, but it may be too uniform — diversifying your fiber sources could improve how efficiently they work together.

fiber_starved.client_summary: Your gut bacteria aren't getting enough fiber from your diet and are turning to your gut's mucus layer as a backup fuel source — this isn't sustainable long-term.

pattern_A_high_fiber_low_cross.guild_metrics: Fiber Degraders >30%, high redundancy + Cross-Feeders <10%, low redundancy
pattern_A_high_fiber_low_cross.likely_diet: Single-fiber source dominance OR rapidly fermented fiber excess
examples: Daily inulin supplement but limited dietary fiber diversity
pattern_A_high_fiber_low_cross.intervention: Diversify fiber types: add resistant starch, slowly fermented fibers, rotate sources

pattern_B_protein_over_fiber.guild_metrics: Proteolytic >15% + Fiber Degraders <20% + CUR likely negative
pattern_B_protein_over_fiber.likely_diet: High protein:fiber ratio OR little fiber reaches colon
pattern_B_protein_over_fiber.intervention: Increase total fiber (target >30g/day), emphasize slowly digested fibers

redundancy_pattern_low_diversity.guild_metrics: Fiber J <0.50 AND Cross-Feeders J <0.40 AND Butyrate J <0.50
redundancy_pattern_low_diversity.likely_diet: Low dietary diversity — repeated same foods
redundancy_pattern_low_diversity.intervention: Rotate vegetables weekly (20+ plants/week), vary grain and protein sources

b_vitamin_pathways_low.pattern: Multiple B vitamin pathways all <0.001 allocation

dual_interpretation.dietary_sufficiency: Microbes don't need to synthesize — abundant in gut from diet (evolutionary economy)
dual_interpretation.ecosystem_collapse: Microbiome lacks synthesizing organisms — may contribute to deficiency risk

b_vitamin_pathways_low.resolution: Dietary intake assessment REQUIRED to distinguish

scfa_routing_alternative.pattern: PWY-7111 (isobutanol) present but PWY-5676 (classical butyrate) absent despite F.prausnitzii/Roseburia present
scfa_routing_alternative.likely_cause: Acetate limitation due to Cross-Feeder constraints
scfa_routing_alternative.intervention: Increase soluble fiber (acetate-producing substrate), support Cross-Feeder guild

CUR_low_alternatives: Alternative: Fast transit time (adequate fiber not fermenting)
Alternative: Recent antibiotic exposure (selectively eliminated fiber degraders)
Alternative: Malabsorption (carbs absorbed in small intestine)
Alternative: Recent dietary shift not yet reflected (3-7 day lag)
PPR_high_alternatives: Alternative: Low fiber with moderate protein (fiber insufficiency is the driver)
Alternative: Fast transit (protein reaches colon but fiber doesn't ferment)
resolution_strategy: 1. Identify metabolic pattern from CLR ratios
5. Assess relevant history (antibiotics, motility, chronic conditions)
6. Determine most likely cause(s) — often multi-factorial
7. Tailor intervention to actual cause, not assumed cause

practical_workflow: Step 1: Calculate CLR ratios (CUR, FCR, MDR, PPR)
Step 2: Pattern matching against integrated templates
Step 3: Guild-level confirmation (check abundances match ratios)
Step 5: Intervention prioritization based on confirmed cause
limitations: Inference ≠ assessment — cannot confirm actual dietary intake
Individual variation: identical diets may produce different microbiome responses
Transit time affects fermentation regardless of fiber intake
Microbiome reflects recent 3-7 day dietary patterns, not long-term habits
Medications, stress, sleep affect microbiome independent of diet
Cannot infer: specific caloric intake, precise macronutrient ratios, food intolerances/allergies, eating disorders
CAN infer (with caveats): relative fiber intake, protein:fiber ratio, dietary diversity, fiber type patterns, plant:animal ratio
